steps.title: Understand infinitely many solutions
steps.content: An equation has infinitely many solutions when both sides are identical expressions. This means the equation is an identity: it is true for every value of $x$.

steps.title: Match terms to find k
steps.content:
For the equation $6x + 24 = 6x + k$ to be an identity:
  • The coefficient of $x$ is already 6 on both sides ✓
  • The constant terms must also match: the left side has 24, and the right side has $k$
Therefore, $k = 24$.
